seoTitle: Regency Downs vs Reid River — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Regency Downs and Reid River. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Regency Downs vs Reid River: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Regency Downs and Reid River.
heading: Regency Downs vs Reid River — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Regency Downs, Queensland and Reid River, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Regency Downs has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Reid River (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Regency Downs or Reid River safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Regency Downs has a flood risk score of 30/100, while Reid River scores 55/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Regency Downs scores 15/100 for bushfire risk. Reid River scores 47/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Regency Downs or Reid River?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Regency Downs has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.55% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
